storage_collision_risk.description: Chamber uses ERC-7201 namespaced storage slot 0x6859c8... for ChamberStorage. Board uses slot 0xae916a... for BoardStorage. Wallet uses slot 0x471e58... for WalletStorage. No overlap with each other or with OZ base contracts (ERC4626, ReentrancyGuard use their own OZ-defined slots).
Agent uses ERC-7201 slot 0xd17d9a... for AgentStorage. ChamberRegistry uses slot 0xf63155... No overlap.
New fields added to ChamberStorage, BoardStorage, WalletStorage, or AgentStorage MUST be appended to the end of the struct. Reordering or removing fields causes silent storage corruption on upgrade.
storage_collision_risk.action: Maintain append-only discipline for all namespace structs

checks: Initializes ERC4626, ERC20, ReentrancyGuard base contracts
Zero address checks on implementation and admin

upgrade_authorization.authorized_by: Chamber itself (multisig governance via executeTransaction → upgradeImplementation)
upgrade_authorization.note: msg.sender == address(this) check ensures only multisig execution can trigger upgrade

upgrade_authorization.authorized_by: Agent owner (ProxyAdmin owner set to agent owner at creation)
upgrade_authorization.note: Agent owner can upgrade unilaterally — no governance gate. This is by design for individual smart accounts.

upgrade_sequence_concerns.description: If all board nodes become undelegated (e.g., mass NFT burns or all holders undelegate), no directors exist and no governance transactions can be submitted. The Chamber's upgrade path is permanently locked.
upgrade_sequence_concerns.mitigation: Design note: ensure at least one permanent director pathway or admin escape hatch exists for catastrophic recovery
